acceptance_targets: [ch05-f001] PASS if each targeted span reads as complete sentences (subject + finite verb) with no run of two or more clipped fragments; FAIL if any target span still uses stacked fragment beats like "X. Y. Z." for core narration.

[ch05-f002] PASS if the revised 387-417 span contains at least two explicit on-page safety actions (for example: lock checks, patrol coordination, relocation logistics performed) and one embodied fear response before process dialogue dominates; FAIL if it remains mostly advisory talk.

[ch05-f006] PASS if the revised 393-539 section contains one explicit irreversible decision with immediate downside before chapter end and avoids consecutive exposition-heavy dialogue blocks longer than one paragraph without action or reversal; FAIL otherwise.

[F008] Pass if revised lines carry audible pressure (overlap, clipped retorts, differentiated diction) and no longer read as a sequence of formally parallel policy statements; fail if the exchange still scans as institutional transcript prose.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if each targeted span reads as complete sentences (subject + finite verb) with no run of two or more clipped fragments; FAIL if any target span still uses stacked fragment beats like "X. Y. Z." for core narration.
findings.problem: Narration repeatedly uses elliptical fragment chains (e.g., object-only beats and clipped noun phrases). This violates the constitution rule against clipping and reads as an autopilot rhythm tic rather than controlled emphasis.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise clipped clusters at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:181, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:283, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:469, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:515 into grammatically complete sentences with explicit subjects while preserving urgency.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised 387-417 span contains at least two explicit on-page safety actions (for example: lock checks, patrol coordination, relocation logistics performed) and one embodied fear response before process dialogue dominates; FAIL if it remains mostly advisory talk.
findings.problem: Timidity drift after the family-threat beat: once Jalen voices abduction fear, the chapter pivots quickly into legal/process briefing and campaign-vendor intel. The emotional and physical danger is acknowledged but under-rendered before strategy resumes.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:387-417 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:463-479, add concrete household-security action and embodied fear consequences (for Lena and/or Jalen) before returning to legal/procedural discussion.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised 393-539 section contains one explicit irreversible decision with immediate downside before chapter end and avoids consecutive exposition-heavy dialogue blocks longer than one paragraph without action or reversal; FAIL otherwise.
findings.problem: The chapter lands a strong irreversible turn (suspension) but then diffuses momentum through back-to-back legal/process briefings. The final movement prioritizes information transfer over a sharper consequence beat.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress one exposition block and recast the other as active conflict in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:393-535, then end with a concrete irreversible choice tied to suspension consequences.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if revised lines carry audible pressure (overlap, clipped retorts, differentiated diction) and no longer read as a sequence of formally parallel policy statements; fail if the exchange still scans as institutional transcript prose.
findings.problem: Mantra-like policy diction continues here, with formal uncontracted exchange dominating a crisis scene that should show more spoken pressure texture. Voices of Lena, Clay, and city leadership converge too closely.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the city-hall confrontation at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1495, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1503, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1517, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1525, and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1527. Keep factual/legal arguments, but introduce interruption, contraction-heavy stress speech, and character-specific rhetorical habits so leverage shifts feel lived, not scripted.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When edge cases arise, choose the version that preserves uncomfortable specificity and character accountability; do not replace difficult scenes with procedural summary, and do not mute class, race, sexuality, or institutional cruelty when those forces are actively shaping choices.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene with violence, racism, grief, or moral compromise is rendered in abstract or polite shorthand, revise to restore specific bodily, social, and material consequence before accepting the draft.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in contemporary Mississippi speech; uncontracted forms are reserved for formality, emphasis, or social performance.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: rotate structural textures across chapters: procedural surge, quiet domestic interval, document-driven slow burn, dread preparation, aftermath inventory, and information-turn chapters; avoid repeating scene count plus dialogue density pattern in adjacent chapters.
prose_style_profile.diction: literary but grounded; precise legal and forensic terms when needed, balanced by lived vernacular and local material detail.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored primarily to Lena, with brief controlled expansions only when factual clarity requires it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: alternate compressed procedural sentences with longer sensory-emotional lines so pressure and reflection keep rebalancing.
